news.title:
Aarti Industries expands supply deal with global firm, plans Rs 200-250 crore investment for feedstock production.
Project at Dahej SEZ aims to improve cost efficiency and EBITDA margins over 15 years.
news.summary:
Aarti Industries expands supply deal with global firm, plans Rs 200-250 crore investment for feedstock production.
Project at Dahej SEZ aims to improve cost efficiency and EBITDA margins over 15 years.

news.title:
US tariff reduction from 50% to 18% under the 2026 trade deal boosts Indian chemical exports, valued at $5.7B in FY24.
Beneficiaries include Aarti Industries, SRF, UPL, Aether Industries, and Navin Fluorine, all reporting significant YoY revenue growth.
news.summary:
US tariff reduction from 50% to 18% under the 2026 trade deal boosts Indian chemical exports, valued at $5.7B in FY24.
Beneficiaries include Aarti Industries, SRF, UPL, Aether Industries, and Navin Fluorine, all reporting significant YoY revenue growth.

news.title:
Aarti Industries' revenue grew 11% QoQ to Rs. 2,492 Cr, EBITDA up 11% QoQ to Rs. 323 Cr.
Exports contributed 65% of revenue; Rs. 1,100 Cr capex planned for FY26 with advanced projects.
news.summary:
Aarti Industries' revenue grew 11% QoQ to Rs. 2,492 Cr, EBITDA up 11% QoQ to Rs. 323 Cr.
Exports contributed 65% of revenue; Rs. 1,100 Cr capex planned for FY26 with advanced projects.

news.title:
India-US trade deal cuts tariffs on Indian chemical exports to 18% from 50%, boosting competitiveness.
Stocks to watch: Aarti Industries, Navin Fluorine, Gujarat Fluorochemicals, Laxmi Organic, Vinati Organics, SRF Ltd.
news.summary:
India-US trade deal cuts tariffs on Indian chemical exports to 18% from 50%, boosting competitiveness.
Stocks to watch: Aarti Industries, Navin Fluorine, Gujarat Fluorochemicals, Laxmi Organic, Vinati Organics, SRF Ltd.

news.title: Aarti Industries paid full tariff on 40% of US exports, contributing 18% to revenue.
news.summary: Aarti Industries paid full tariff on 40% of US exports, contributing 18% to revenue.

news.title:
Aarti Industries' Q3 EBITDA rose to 3.22B from 2.3B YoY.
EBITDA margin improved to 13.9% from 12.5% YoY.
news.summary:
Aarti Industries' Q3 EBITDA rose to 3.22B from 2.3B YoY.
EBITDA margin improved to 13.9% from 12.5% YoY.

news.title:
Aarti Industries Ltd to hold Q3 FY26 earnings call on Feb 3, 2026 at 12 PM IST.
Management discussion on financial results will precede an interactive Q&A session.
news.summary:

news.title:
Aarti Inds rose 1.87% to Rs 371 after securing long-term Methanol & Toluene supply deals.
These chemicals support AIL's applications in pharma, agrochemicals, FMCG, and industrial sectors.
news.summary:

Contracts with GCC and Southeast Asia ensure supply security and cost visibility.

news.title:
Aarti Industries sees market evaluation revision, reflecting nuanced changes in metrics.
The shift highlights complex financial and technical dynamics amid sector challenges.

news.title:
Aarti Industries fell 1.75% on 5 Dec, closing at 362.9, down 6.45.
The stock continues underperforming its sector and broader market benchmarks.

news.title:
Aarti Industries faces Rs. 1.97 cr duty demand, Rs. 0.38 cr penalty.
The company plans to appeal, citing no material financial impact.
news.summary:
Aarti Industries faces Rs. 1.97 cr duty demand, Rs. 0.38 cr penalty.

news.title:
HDFC Securities gives 'Add' rating to Somany, J Kumar, Aarti, Aster, Hitachi, Torrent; 'Buy' for Amber, MCX, Lupin, Cummins, Apollo.
Torrent Pharma projected for steady growth via branded franchise, launches, Brazil traction, US generics turnaround, and stable margins.

news.title:

Signed chlorine supply deal to secure raw materials; working on cost optimization and US tariff mitigation.
news.summary:

news.title:
Aarti Industries Q2 net profit rose to 1.06B vs 520M YoY, revenue up to 21B vs 16.28B.
EBITDA climbed to 2.92B vs 1.97B YoY; margin improved to 13.9% from 12.1%.
news.summary:
Aarti Industries Q2 net profit rose to 1.06B vs 520M YoY, revenue up to 21B vs 16.28B.
EBITDA climbed to 2.92B vs 1.97B YoY; margin improved to 13.9% from 12.1%.

news.title:

Management to discuss results on Nov 6, followed by Q&A session; dial-in details provided.
